meaning: adj.情报的,提供情报的,见闻广博的
examples: The text was informative and well written.
Burton's text is concise and informative.
The talk was both informative and entertaining.
It said its vision of TV everywhere will be more personal, social, ubiquitous and informative.
In place of informative, reasoned opinion, powerful because of its logic and humaneness, we have sentiment.
